source_jobs.title: Founding Deployed Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT DELFA

Billions of people still suffer from every-day diseases.

One key reason? Clinical trials are broken.

Despite $120 billion spent each year, most trials are delayed due to bottlenecks like patient recruitment.

Delfa is fixing this with autonomous AI agents. We're building AI agents to automate clinical trials.

In less than a year from launch we've already:

 - Deployed onto 300+ trials across multiple conditions

 - Reached seven figures in ARR, growing 22% MoM

 - Raised an oversubscribed $3.8m seed from tier-one AI investors


OVERVIEW

Delfa is at a critical inflection: we have a proven offering which is on track to support the recruitment of thousands of participants into trials in 2026, while simultaneously expanding our offerings with new products for biopharmaceutical companies.

We are hiring a Founding Deployed Engineer to own the conversion of pilots, success of new customers and identification of new product opportunities. You will be responsible for deeply understanding our customer needs and working shoulder-to-shoulder to solve them.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own the pilot-to-customer conversion process end-to-end, from onboarding, value quantification through to expansion

 - Build scalable onboarding processes and playbooks that can work across a growing customer base

 - Deploy AI agents to handle onboarding grunt work and free up time for high-leverage customer interactions

 - Work directly with customers to extract validated product insights and quickly prototype solutions to test them

 - Spot patterns across the customer base and package them up for engineering so the right things get built

 - Help open and grow our US presence as we expand our commercial team there




YOU'LL BE A GREAT FIT IF…

 - You are technical and commercial in equal measure: You're comfortable writing code to prototype a solution and equally comfortable sitting in front of a customer explaining why it matters. The bridge between those two worlds is where you live.

 - You are obsessed with feedback loops: You understand that in a world where code is cheap, the real bottleneck is extracting validated ideas from customers fast. You build systems and habits to make that happen.

 - You thrive in complexity: Clinical trial sites are regulated, risk-averse organisations with high stakes. You know how to build trust with them and how to show them value in a way that meets them where they are.

 - You don't wait for instruction: You identify the problem, define the solution, and move. When you see a pattern across three customers, you've already written the proposal before anyone asks you to.

 - You love using AI: You reach for Claude, Cursor, or whatever the right tool is to ship faster. You're experimenting constantly and sharing what you learn.




LIFE AT DELFA

Delfa is building a generational company to transform the way medications reach millions of patients in need.

This is your opportunity to get in on the ground floor and work alongside the highest talent density team in the space, onsite at our London office 5 days a week:

 - A wine-snob who built foundation models at Roche at ETH Zurich

 - A former hedge fund trader with world class causal AI knowledge

 - A medical doctor turned AI engineer who builds prototypes on the weekend for fun

 - A ginger bloke who deployed production AI for J&J, Medtronic, and Exscientia




WHAT WE OFFER

 - Competitive salary + meaningful equity

 - Private health insurance and gym membership

 - 25 days' holiday + UK public holidays

 - Modern office space 5 mins from Old Street station

 - True 0 → 1 ownership.




OUR HIRING PROCESS

We work to get you an offer within a week with a lean process:

 - Intro chat with Tom (CEO) - 30 mins: A discussion about your experience, values, and career goals, and an opportunity to get a sense of the role and the team. After this call we may ask you to share some work you're proud of.

 - Case Study Interview - 90 mins: A deeper dive into h

source_jobs.title: AI Agent Operations Specialist (FTE 60%)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT DELFA

Billions of people still suffer from every-day diseases.

One key reason? Clinical trials are broken.

Despite $120 billion spent each year, most trials are delayed due to bottlenecks like patient recruitment.

Delfa is fixing this with autonomous AI agents. We're building AI agents to automate clinical trials.

In less than a year from launch we’ve already:

 - Deployed onto 300+ trials across multiple conditions

 - Reached seven figures in ARR, growing 25% MoM

 - Raised an oversubscribed $3.8m seed from tier-one AI investors


WHAT YOU'LL DO

You’ll help design, review, test, and continuously improve the AI agents that interact with patients and support clinical trial sites every day.

This role sits at the intersection of clinical operations, AI agent design, quality assurance, and customer delivery. You’ll use AI tools extensively, but your judgment, attention to detail, and ability to spot issues will be what makes the work reliable.

You will:

 - Use Delfa’s AI workflows to extract inclusion/exclusion criteria from clinical study protocols, then manually review and refine the outputs for accuracy

 - Turn clinical eligibility criteria into clear, patient-friendly screening questions suitable for voice and SMS agents

 - Create and review voice agent reports that describe the agent’s screening logic, conversation flow, escalation rules, and operational setup

 - Support script creation for IRB/ethics submissions, adapting agent scripts to customer templates while preserving clinical and operational accuracy

 - Assist with AI agent management, including manual patches and configuration updates where API-based changes are not yet suitable

 - Sample real calls and patient interactions to identify issues, test fixes, and add recurring items to the monitoring list to inform future automated QA

 - Support pre-release testing of AI agents before they go live with patients

 - Once comfortable, design new AI agents end-to-end, which will then be reviewed and tested by senior team members

 - Work closely with product, engineering, and customer-facing teams to ensure agents are safe, accurate, and operationally useful for clinical research sites


YOU'LL BE A GREAT FIT IF…

You have attention to detail. You’re comfortable reviewing dense clinical or operational documents and can spot when something is missing, unclear, or inconsistent.

You are comfortable using AI tools. You don’t need to be an engineer, but you should be excited to use prompts, structured workflows, and internal tools to produce high-quality work faster.

You are clinically or scientifically literate. Experience with clinical trials, healthcare, life sciences, medicine, nursing, pharmacy, psychology, or biomedical sciences would be valuable.

You care about quality. Our agents interact with real patients and support real clinical trials, so accuracy matters. You take pride in work that is careful, clear, and reliable.

You can move quickly without being careless. Delfa is growing fast, and we need people who can learn quickly, make good judgments, and improve the process as they go.

You have an ownership mindset. When you notice something is broken, unclear, or inefficient, you don’t just flag it, you help fix it.

You communicate clearly. You can explain complex clinical or operational logic in simple, structured language that customers, internal teams, and AI agents can all understand.

Nice to have:

 - Experience in clinical trials, patient recruitment, site operations, IRB/ethics submissions, or healthcare operations

 - Familiarity with inclusion/exclusion criteria, study protocols, or prescreening workflows

 - Experience in QA, call review, operations, or process improvement

 - Comfort working with structured documents, spreadsheets, or configuration tools


LIFE AT DELFA

Delfa is building a generational company to transform the way medications reach millions of patients in need.

This is your opportunity to get in on the ground flo

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT DELFA

Billions of people still suffer from every-day diseases.

One key reason? Clinical trials are broken.

Despite $120 billion spent each year, most trials are delayed due to bottlenecks like patient recruitment.

Delfa is fixing this with autonomous AI agents. We're building AI agents to automate clinical trials.

In less than a year from launch we’ve already:

 - Deployed onto 300+ trials across multiple conditions

 - Reached seven figures in ARR, growing 25% MoM

 - Raised an oversubscribed $3.8m seed from tier-one AI investors




WHAT YOU'LL DO

You'll build and scale the core systems that enable Delfa's AI agents to deliver real-world clinical impact.

 - Design, build, and scale AI agent systems end-to-end: from voice interactions to intelligent workflow orchestration across trial sites

 - Own complex projects from architecture to deployment, with real autonomy over technical decisions

 - Build and evolve the infrastructure, observability, and monitoring systems needed to ensure quality and reliability at scale

 - Integrate leading-edge models from AI labs into production workflows, evaluating and adopting new capabilities as the frontier moves

 - Design robust data pipelines for ingesting, transforming, and serving high-quality healthcare data

 - Continuously improve internal AI tooling and platform to unlock new use cases and increase team velocity

Our current tech stack:

FastAPI · Pydantic · TypeScript · React · OpenAI / Anthropic · Temporal · PostgreSQL · Honeycomb · PlanetScale · Azure




YOU'LL BE A GREAT FIT IF…

 - You have 3+ years of professional full-stack engineering experience: We're looking for somebody comfortable with solving hard technical problems, refactoring when needed, and knowing what's enough and what's too much to solve a problem.

 - A track record of building high-quality products fast: Delfa's got big ambitions to materially accelerate getting life saving treatments to market. This is necessary and urgent work so we need individuals who can deliver.

 - An ownership mindset: We position our team to understand customer problems, give you the autonomy to find the best path forward, and expect you to own the solutions.

 - A growth mindset: We're looking for people who take pride in their work and are always looking to improve it. If your work doesn't matter to you, it won't end up mattering to our customers. If you're not growing, your impact won't grow either.




LIFE AT DELFA

Delfa is building a generational company to transform the way medications reach millions of patients in need.

This is your opportunity to get in on the ground floor and work alongside the highest talent density team in the space, onsite at our London office 5 days a week:

 - A wine-snob who built foundation models at Roche at ETH Zurich

 - A former hedge fund trader with world class causal AI knowledge

 - A medical doctor turned AI engineer who builds prototypes on the weekend for fun

 - A ginger bloke who deployed production AI for J&J, Medtronic, and Exscientia




WHAT WE OFFER

 - Competitive salary + meaningful equity

 - Private health insurance and gym membership

 - 25 days’ holiday + UK public holidays

 - Modern office space 5 mins from Old Street station

 - True 0 → 1 ownership. You’ll take full responsibility for critical product features that get new treatments to patients, faster.




OUR HIRING PROCESS

We work to get you an offer within a week with the following lean process:

 1. Intro chat with https://www.linkedin.com/in/farrandtom/%5CGustav https://www.linkedin.com/in/gustavbredell/ (CTO) - 30 mins: An informal discussion about your experiences, values, and career goals, and give you an opportunity to get a sense of the role and the team. After this call we may ask you to share some work you’re proud of.

You communicate clearly. You can explain complex clinical or operational logic in simple, structured language that customers, internal teams, and AI agents can all understand.

This is your opportunity to get in on the ground floor and work alongside the highest talent density team in the space, onsite at our London office 5 days a week:
